message:
feat(tui): add filter preset save/load system to Messages and Reservations screens

Implement persistent filter preset dialogs (Ctrl+S to save, Ctrl+L to
load) for the Messages and Reservations TUI screens, bringing them to
feature parity with the Timeline screen's existing preset support.

Messages screen (tui_screens/messages.rs):
- Add ScreenFilterPresetStore integration with on-disk persistence via
  tui_persist infrastructure (screen_filter_presets.json).
- New PresetDialogMode enum (None/Save/Load) controls overlay state.
- SavePresetField enum (Name/Description) with Tab cycling between
  fields in the save dialog.
- snapshot_filter_values() captures current query text, preset_index,
  and inbox_mode (Local project slug or Global) into a BTreeMap.
- apply_preset_values() restores query, preset index, and inbox mode
  from a saved BTreeMap, with fallback logic for missing keys.
- save_named_preset() / apply_named_preset() / remove_named_preset()
  manage the full CRUD lifecycle against the preset store.
- handle_save_dialog_key() / handle_load_dialog_key() process keyboard
  input within the modal overlays (Esc, Tab, Enter, Delete, j/k nav).
- render_splitter_handle() adds visual pane separation in the
  messages layout when terminal width exceeds MESSAGE_SPLIT_GAP_THRESHOLD.
- Constructor refactored to build() with optional path override for
  test isolation; with_filter_presets_path_for_test() for unit tests.

Reservations screen (tui_screens/reservations.rs):
- Mirror implementation of the preset system from Messages, adapted
  for reservation-specific filter state (sort_col, sort_asc,
  show_released as the persisted filter dimensions).
- Add centered_overlay_rect() helper for dialog positioning.
- render_save_preset_dialog() / render_load_preset_dialog() render
  the modal overlays with themed styling.
- Ctrl+S/Ctrl+L keybindings intercept before other key handlers;
  preset dialog captures all input when active.
- Updated keybindings() and context_help_tip() to document the new
  Ctrl+S/Ctrl+L shortcuts.
- Constructor refactored identically to Messages for test isolation.

Both screens share the same on-disk preset file but use distinct
screen IDs ("messages" / "reservations") as namespace keys.

Co-Authored-By: Claude Opus 4.6 <noreply@anthropic.com>
